text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 1]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Q^2 / (d^4 * Pp) = f(mu / (p*sigma*d))

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1. Details of Atomizer of Standard shape**

    | Header                        | Data        |
    | ----------------------------- | ----------- |
    | Diameter of final orifice      | d           |
    | Diameter of tangential ports   | p=d         |
    | Length of final orifice    | t = 2d/3          |
    | Diameter of swirl chamber    | D = 6d to 9d          |
    | Length of cylindrical part of swirl chamber          | b = 2d          |
    | Radius of offset of tangential ports           | r = D/2 - 3d/4         |
    | Angle of cone of swirl chamber | phi = 60 to 90 deg.     |

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 1. Arrangements of Atomizer**

    *   **Components and Labels:**
        *   Arrangement a: Internal threads for fuel line attachment.
        *   Arrangement b: Orifice plate and back plug design.
        *   Arrangement c: Detailed view showing key dimensions and the conic swirl chamber.
        *   b: Length of cylindrical part of swirl chamber.
        *   D: Diameter of swirl chamber.
        *   d: Diameter of final orifice.
        *   p: Diameter of tangential ports.
        *   r: Radius of offset of tangential ports.
        *   t: Length of final orifice.
        *   phi :  Angle of cone of swirl chamber.

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   D is fixed at 7.5d on standard shape
*   phi is fixed at 90 deg on standard shape.

title: 1955_radcliffe_performance_type_of_swirl_atomizer
text:

Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Q = Flow rate
*   P = Pressure
*   d = Diameter of orifice
*   Rho = Density of fuel
*   Mu = Viscosity
* Q2/d4Pp
* Q/ud

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig 2a. Q2/d4Pp Plotted Against Q/ud for Several Liquids (Atomizer 3)**
    *   X-axis: (Q/μd) x 10^-3
    *   Y-axis: (Q2/d4Pp) x 10^-7
    *   Curves: Data points for:
        *   Kerosene
        *   Petrol
        *   Carbon tetrachloride
        *   One part of D.T.D. 44D to one part of kerosene.
        *   Three parts of D.T.D. 44D to one part of kerosene.
        *   Seven parts of D.T.D. 44D to one part of kerosene.
        *   Fifteen parts of D.T.D. 44D to one part of kerosene.
*   **Fig 2b. Q2/d4Pp Plotted Against Q/ud for Several Liquids (Atomizer 6)**
    *   X-axis: (Q/μd) x 10^-3
    *   Y-axis: (Q2/d4Pp) x 10^-7
         *   Curves: Data points for:
            *   Kerosene
            *   Petrol
            *   Carbon tetrachloride
            *   One part of D.T.D. 44D to one part of kerosene.
            *   Three parts of D.T.D. 44D to one part of kerosene.
            *   Seven parts of D.T.D. 44D to one part of kerosene.
            *   Fifteen parts of D.T.D. 44D to one part of kerosene.

*   **Fig. 3. Relation Between Q2/d4Pp and Q/μd (Consistent Units)**
    *   X-axis: Q/μd
    *   Y-axis: Q2/d4Pp
    *   Scale: Logarithmic

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 2. Parameters Controlling Flow in Swirl Atomizers**
    *   Headers: Parameter, Symbol, Dimension

        *   Flow rate, Q, M/T
        *   Pressure, P, M/LT2
        *   Diameter of orifice, d, L

*   **Table 3. Dimensions of Atomizers of Similar Shape (Measurements in inches.)**

text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 9]
Here's the breakdown of the technical content from the provided image:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   √(Q^2 / d^4Pp) and Re. (Q/μd)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Figure 16: Discharge Coefficient of Nineteen Atomizers at 700 lb. per sq. in.**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis:  `4Ap/πDd` (unitless)
        *   Y-axis: Discharge Coefficient, K (unitless)
    *   Curves:
        *   Data points representing experimental results.
        *   Curve labeled "Curve Given by Inviscid-Flow Theory"
    *   Key Values: x-axis range from 0.0 to 1.0, y-axis range from 0.0 to 0.4
*   **Figure 17: Discharge Coefficient at Same Re.**
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: `4Ap/πDd` (unitless)
        *   Y-axis: Discharge Coefficient, K (unitless)
    *   Curves:
        *   Data points representing experimental results (scatter plot).
        *   Curve labeled "Curve Given by Inviscid-Flow Theory"
    *   Key Values: x-axis range from 0.0 to 1.0, y-axis range from 0.0 to 0.4
*   **Figure 18: Deviation of K from Ideal Curve Plotted Against Ratio of Swirl Chamber Diameter to Orifice Diameter, D/d** *alb is the ratio of measured K to K from ideal curve*
    *   Axes:
        *   X-axis: D/d (unitless)
        *   Y-axis: a/b (unitless)
    *   Curves:

        *   Mean line

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   70 to 107 degrees (spray angle)
*   5 to 700 lb. per sq. in. (pressure range)
*   D/d ratios > 10 (for some atomizers)
*   D should not be greater than 5d
*   orifice length can be d/5
*   Specific Gravity of kerosene : 0.8
*   Flow number: from 0.1 to 9.25
*   279 atomizer combinations
*   72 swirl ports approximating tangential
*   19 atomizers

Here's the breakdown of technical content:

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   **Fig. 19: K Corrected for D/d Ratio (K') Plotted Against 4*Delta p/(pi*D*d)**
    *   Axes: x: 4*Delta p/(pi*D*d), y: K'
    *   Units: dimensionless for both axes
    *   Curve: Data points with a line representing inviscid-flow theory.

*   **Fig. 20: Proportional Deviation of K' from Ideal Curve Plotted Against Ratio L/D**
    *   Axes: x: L/D, y: Deviation of K'
    *   Units: dimensionless for both axes
    *   Curve: Data points with a fitted curve.
    *   NOTE: L = Total length of swirl chamber (written below the graph)

*   **Fig. 21: Final Corrected K (K") Plotted Against 4*Delta p/(pi*D*d)**
    *   Axes: x: 4*Delta p/(pi*D*d), y: Deviation of K"
    *   Units: dimensionless for both axes
    *   Curve: Data points with a line representing inviscid-flow theory.
    *   NOTE: K" is K' corrected for L/D ratio (written below the graph)

*   **Fig. 22: Plot of Measured Spray Angle Against Measured Discharge Coefficient**
    *   Axes: x: Measured K, y: Spray Angle
    *   Units: x: dimensionless, y: degrees
    *   Curve: Data with a implied curve.

*   **Fig. 23: Deviation of Spray Angle from Ideal Curve Plotted Against K * d/2**
    *   Axes: x: K * (d/2) * 10^2, y: Deviation of Spray Angle
    *   Units: x: unknown, y: degrees
    *   Curve: Data, roughly linear trend.

**3. Tables:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   D/d ratio = 3.5 (ideal value, mentioned in text)
*   L: Total length of swivel chamber
*   K: Discharge coefficient
*   K': K corrected for D/d ratio
*   K": K' (corrected for D/d ratio) corrected for L/D ratio
*   Delta p: Pressure drop
*   D, d: Diameters (likely swirl chamber and orifice, respectively, but undefined)
*   Re: Reynolds Number (mentioned in text)

title: 1955_radcliffe_performance_type_of_swirl_atomizer
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 12]
Here's the breakdown of the technical content from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Q2/d4P  (used for a dimensionless parameter with dimensions (lb. or gramme) x (second2 or hour²) x (cm.3 or in.2x feet).
*   Ur² = constant
*   Kq = (π /4) * Q / (p² d² sqrt(2g/ρ) p)
*   Kq² = Phi(Kq*Re)
*   FN= Q / (p^(1/2)* V * P) = Kq d² sqrt(2g) /P^(1/2)

**2. Graphs / Charts:**

*   **Figure 28:**
    *   **Axes:** x = Re (Reynolds number), y= Kq (Discharge Coefficient)
    *   **Units:** Not explicitly stated on x or y axis in the image, but Re is typically dimensionless, and Kq is also dimensionless.
    *   **Curves:** A single curve showing a generally decreasing trend of Kq as Re increases.
    *   **Key Values:** At the lowest flow, Kq=0.66.  Kq varied between 0.4 and 0.25.

**3. Tables**
*   NO_CONTENT


**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Figure 27: Theoretical Spray Angle**
    *   Components: Diagram of a theoretical spray, showing the liquid sheet boundary.

    *   Labels:
        *   THEORETICAL ANGLE
        *   ANGLE OF SPRAY CURTAIN

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   p²/2rd = 0.167  (constant for 'standard' atomizers)
*   Theoretical discharge coefficient = 0.13 (corresponding to p²/2rd = 0.167)
*   Lowest flow Kq=0.66

title: 1955_radcliffe_performance_type_of_swirl_atomizer
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 13]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   `d = 220P^-0.458Q^0.209ν^0.215`
*   `S ∝ R_t^-0.215 W^-0.115 R_G^-0.251`
*   `S ∝ R_t^-0.211 W^-0.257`
*   `Q = A d^0.94 p^0.70 r^-0.84`
*   `Q = A_1 d^1.21 p^0.61 r^-0.24`

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 7. S.M.D., MICRONS**
    *   Headers:
        *   P, lb. per sq. in.
        *   Q, lb. per hr.
    *   Data:

| P, lb. per sq. in. | Q, lb. per hr. |
| --- | --- |
|  | 2 | 200 |
|  | A | B | A | B |
| 50 | 45 | 52 | 143 | 135 |
| 1,000 | 14 | 13 | 43 | 35 |

**4. Diagrams:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   d = Sauter mean diameter, in microns
*   P = pressure drop, in lb. per sq. in.
*   Q = mass flow, in lb. per hr.
*   v_f = kinematic viscosity of fuel, in centistokes
*   v_G = kinematic viscosity of gas, in centistokes
*   ρ_o = gas density, in grammes per cu. cm.
*   γ_t = surface tension, in dynes per cm.
*   F = flow number, in gal. per hr. per lb. per sq. in.
*   S = S(σ*d / F_t) = non-dimensional form for drop size
*   R_t = (σ * P^2 * F_t / v_f) = Reynolds number for fuel
*   R_G = (σ * P^2 * F_t / v_G) = Reynolds number for the gas
*   W = (σ * ρ_o * P * F_t^2 / γ) = Webers number based on the gas density.
*   v_f = 2.3 centistokes
*   For atomizer of more usual shape: `d = 0.42 inch, p= 0.52 inch, r = 0-96 inch`
*   P= 3,520 lb. per sq. in.
*   v_f = 5 centistokes.
*   Hole diameter = 0.38 mm.
*   Calculated S.M.D. = 21.2 microns
*   hole length 0.4 mm. = 21.3 microns
*   hole length 0.9 mm. = 17.9 microns
